model_narrative: A farm in the Agreste region plans a single season. It decides how many hectares of each cropping activity to run on each land type, how much livestock to keep under different feeding alternatives, how much of each crop to sell versus consume on-farm, how much family / temporary / permanent labor to use in each month, and how much land to downgrade between types. The goal is to maximize farm income.

sets.name: ps
sets.doc: set of feasible (cropping-activity p, land-type s) pairs (the activity/land combinations with positive crop yield)

params.name: rations
params.doc: livestock rations per feed alternative (cr per head)

params.name: lio
params.doc: land required per livestock feed alternative (ha per head)

params.name: llab
params.doc: labor for livestock feed (man-days per head)

params.name: crev
params.doc: crop revenue time series (cr per ha)

params.name: yield_param
params.doc: crop yields (kg per ha) — Pyomo attribute is model.yield_param ('yield' is a Python keyword)

params.name: ravg
params.doc: average crop revenue = mean over ty of crev(c,ty)

params.name: prdev
params.doc: price deviations for crops (cr per ton)

params.name: pcost
params.doc: cropping technology cost = sum over km of techc(p,km)

params.name: a
params.doc: cropping constant (0 if activity grows cotton-h, else 1)

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: cropping activities (ha); ub xcropl where set

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: revenue from crop and livestock sales (cr)

description: For each land type, the total area used cannot exceed the area of that type that is available. Land gets used in three ways: by growing crops on it, which only applies to land types suitable for cropping, by land that has been downgraded or converted in from another land type, and by land set aside to feed livestock.
expected_pyomo:
def landb_rule(model, s):
    crop_term = 0
    if s in model.sc:
        crop_term = sum(model.a[p] * model.xcrop[p,s] for p in model.p if (p,s) in model.ps)
    downgrade_term = sum(model.ldp[s,sp] * model.lswitch[sp] for sp in model.s)
    livestock_term = sum(model.lio[s,r] * model.xliver[r] for r in model.r)
    return crop_term + downgrade_term + livestock_term <= model.landc[s]
model.landb = Constraint(model.s, rule=landb_rule)

description: For each crop, the quantity produced must be at least the quantity used. Production is the crop's yield gathered across all cropping activities and land types, and usage is the amount of that crop sold plus the amount the family consumes through its consumption bundles.
expected_pyomo:
def mbalc_rule(model, c):
    production = sum(model.yield_param[p,c,s] * model.xcrop[p,s] for p in model.p for s in model.s) / 1000
    consumption = model.sales[c] + sum(model.cbndl[c,dr] * model.cons[dr] for dr in model.dr)
    return production >= consumption
model.mbalc = Constraint(model.c, rule=mbalc_rule)

description: For each month, the labor required cannot exceed the labor available that month. Labor is required by cropping activities, based on each activity's monthly labor need per hectare applied over the land it occupies, and by livestock feeding. The labor available that month is family labor plus temporary labor, plus permanent labor converted into man-days.
expected_pyomo:
def labc_rule(model, tm):
    crop_labor = sum(model.labor[p,tm] * model.xcrop[p,s] for (p,s) in model.ps)
    livestock_labor = sum(model.llab[tm,r] * model.xliver[r] for r in model.r)
    available_labor = model.flab[tm] + model.tlab[tm] + model.dpm * model.plab
    return crop_labor + livestock_labor <= available_labor
model.labc = Constraint(model.tm, rule=labc_rule)

The revenue effect is each crop's price deviation for the year times the amount of that crop sold, summed over crops.

description: The farm's cash spending during the season cannot exceed the available working capital. The cash items are cropping cost, livestock ration cost, veterinary cost, the wages paid for temporary labor across the months, and the wages paid for permanent labor. Family labor is not a cash outlay, so it is excluded here.

description: Farm income is revenue, plus the value of the food the family consumes itself, minus labor cost, ration cost, veterinary cost, and cropping cost, minus a risk penalty proportional to the average size of the yearly price deviations, counting positive and negative together, taken across all the years.
expected_pyomo: model.income = Constraint(expr=model.yfarm == model.revenue + model.vsc * sum(model.cons[dr] for dr in model.dr) - model.labcost - model.rationr - model.vetcost - model.cropcost - model.phi * sum(model.pdev[ty] + model.ndev[ty] for ty in model.ty) / len(model.ty))

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: cropping activities (ha); ub xcropl where set

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: revenue from crop and livestock sales (cr)

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, for each land type the total area used cannot exceed the area of that type that is available, where land is used by growing crops on the types suitable for cropping, by land converted in from another type, and by land set aside to feed livestock. Second, the total livestock raised equals the herd kept summed across all the feeding alternatives. Third, the farm's ration requirement equals the per-head ration cost of each feeding alternative times the herd kept on it, summed over the alternatives. Fourth, for each crop the quantity produced must be at least the quantity used, where production is the crop's yield gathered across all cropping activities and land types, and usage is the amount sold plus the amount the family eats through its consumption bundles. Fifth, for each crop the total production in tons equals that same yield summed over every cropping activity and land type. Sixth, for each month the labor required cannot exceed the labor available, where the requirement comes from cropping activities scaled by each activity's monthly labor need and the land it occupies plus the work of feeding livestock, and the availability is family labor plus temporary labor plus permanent labor converted into man-days. Seventh, the family's on-farm consumption across the bundle alternatives must add up to exactly one full bundle. Eighth, for each year the revenue effect of crop price deviations is the difference between an up-deviation amount and a down-deviation amount, each being the crop's price deviation that year times the amount sold, summed over crops. Ninth, total revenue is livestock sales income, the livestock price times the head sold, plus crop sales income, each crop's reference price times the amount sold summed over crops. Tenth, total cropping cost is each cropping activity's per-hectare technology cost times the hectares grown on each land type, summed over every feasible activity and land pairing. Eleventh, total labor cost is family and temporary labor each at its own wage summed over the months and converted from man-days to man-months, plus permanent labor at its annual wage. Twelfth, seasonal cash spending cannot exceed the available working capital, where the cash items are cropping cost, ration cost, veterinary cost, and the temporary and permanent wages, while unpaid family labor is excluded. Thirteenth, total veterinary cost is the per-head veterinary cost times the number of livestock. Finally, farm income is revenue plus the value of the food the family consumes itself, minus labor, ration, veterinary, and cropping cost, minus a risk penalty proportional to the average size of the yearly price deviations, counting ups and downs together.
expected_pyomo:
def landb_rule(model, s):
    crop_term = 0
    if s in model.sc:
        crop_term = sum(model.a[p] * model.xcrop[p,s] for p in model.p if (p,s) in model.ps)
    downgrade_term = sum(model.ldp[s,sp] * model.lswitch[sp] for sp in model.s)
    livestock_term = sum(model.lio[s,r] * model.xliver[r] for r in model.r)
    return crop_term + downgrade_term + livestock_term <= model.landc[s]
model.landb = Constraint(model.s, rule=landb_rule)

model.lbal = Constraint(expr=model.xlive == sum(model.xliver[r] for r in model.r))

model.rliv = Constraint(expr=model.rationr == sum(model.rations[r] * model.xliver[r] for r in model.r))

def mbalc_rule(model, c):
    production = sum(model.yield_param[p,c,s] * model.xcrop[p,s] for p in model.p for s in model.s) / 1000
    consumption = model.sales[c] + sum(model.cbndl[c,dr] * model.cons[dr] for dr in model.dr)
    return production >= consumption
model.mbalc = Constraint(model.c, rule=mbalc_rule)

def dprod_rule(model, c):
    return model.xprod[c] == sum(model.yield_param[p,c,s] * model.xcrop[p,s] for p in model.p for s in model.s) / 1000
model.dprod = Constraint(model.c, rule=dprod_rule)

def labc_rule(model, tm):
    crop_labor = sum(model.labor[p,tm] * model.xcrop[p,s] for (p,s) in model.ps)
    livestock_labor = sum(model.llab[tm,r] * model.xliver[r] for r in model.r)
    available_labor = model.flab[tm] + model.tlab[tm] + model.dpm * model.plab
    return crop_labor + livestock_labor <= available_labor
model.labc = Constraint(model.tm, rule=labc_rule)

model.cond = Constraint(expr=sum(model.cons[dr] for dr in model.dr) == 1)

def ddev_rule(model, ty):
    return sum(model.prdev[c,ty] * model.sales[c] for c in model.c) == model.pdev[ty] - model.ndev[ty]
model.ddev = Constraint(model.ty, rule=ddev_rule)

model.arev = Constraint(expr=model.revenue == model.lprice * model.xlive + 1000 * sum(model.price[c] * model.sales[c] for c in model.c))

model.acrop = Constraint(expr=model.cropcost == sum(model.pcost[p] * model.xcrop[p,s] for (p,s) in model.ps))

model.alab = Constraint(expr=model.labcost == (model.fwage * sum(model.flab[tm] for tm in model.tm) + model.twage * sum(model.tlab[tm] for tm in model.tm)) / model.dpm + model.pwage * model.plab)

model.awcc = Constraint(expr=model.cropcost + model.rationr + model.vetcost + model.twage/model.dpm * sum(model.tlab[tm] for tm in model.tm) + model.pwage * model.plab <= model.wcbar)

model.avet = Constraint(expr=model.vetcost == model.vetpr * model.xlive)

model.income = Constraint(expr=model.yfarm == model.revenue + model.vsc * sum(model.cons[dr] for dr in model.dr) - model.labcost - model.rationr - model.vetcost - model.cropcost - model.phi * sum(model.pdev[ty] + model.ndev[ty] for ty in model.ty) / len(model.ty))
